mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals strong labor market demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates inflationary demand pressures, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 signals robust manufacturing activity, improving corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110, or the signal's IC falls below 0.04 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly federal funds rate above 3% signals tightening monetary policy, increasing borrowing costs and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when both HOUST and RSAFS exceed thresholds, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions, increasing wage pressures and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when UNRATE is below 3.5%, or the signal's IC falls below 0.03 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 2.5% signals persistent inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04 in rolling 2-year windows.
